tasks.question: Where does login start?
tasks.answer_preview:
Answer
  78% confidence · 13ms · auth-standard

  No production login in core framework. Example implementation: `examples/auth/index.js` (see GET/POST /login routes).

tasks.question: What breaks if application changes?
tasks.answer_preview:
Answer
  92% confidence · 12ms · impact-standard

  Changing **lib/application.js** affects **123** nodes (forward + reverse).
  
  **Dependencies / blast radius:**
  • APIs affected: 0
  • Files affected: 122
  • Domains affected: 1
  • Tests affected: 0
  
  **Direct dependents (8):**
  • index.js
  • lib/view.js
  • lib/utils.js
  • lib/express.js
  • lib/request.js
  • lib/response.js
  • l

tasks.question: Find the most critical subsystem
tasks.answer_preview:
Answer
  85% confidence · 14ms · critical-standard

  **Lib** is the most critical general-purpose core subsystem in this repository. Risk score: **100/100** (issues: 6 circular dependencies, 3 dead modules, 1 high-severity smell).
  
  It anchors the dependency graph: every change to its API propagates through the framework. Key modules include the application factory plus request/response pip

tasks.answer_preview:

  Capability keywords: web_framework, middleware, router, application, request.

  Related: HTTP Framework & Middleware
